channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police Confront Armed Attackers Outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, One Dead
item.description: A gunfight erupted outside the high-rise building housing the Israeli consulate in Istanbul on Tuesday, resulting in the death of one attacker and injuries to two others. Police responded rapidly to t...

item.title: SiR to perform live in Johannesburg this May for 'Once Upon a Time in Joburg' event
item.description: SiR, the American singer known for his R&B and neo-soul hits, is set to headline the 'Once Upon a Time in Joburg' event this May. The concert, scheduled for May 23, is expected to be a highlight of th...

item.title: Cricket NSW Supports David Warner Following Drink-Driving Charge
item.description: Cricket New South Wales (NSW) has publicly stated its support for David Warner after the former Australian cricketer was charged with drink-driving. Warner, aged 39, is scheduled to appear in court on...

item.title: Senator Dela Rosa Faces Ethics Complaint Amid Prolonged Absence from Senate
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” Dela Rosa is currently facing scrutiny for having the highest number of absences among senators during the 20th Congress. According to Senate records, he’s missed a significant 3...

item.title: PCSO confirms no winners for April 8 major lotto draws
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no one won the jackpot prizes from the major lotto draws conducted on Wednesday, April 8, 2026. The Grand Lotto 6/55 jackpot, which was ...

item.title: Sweden Tries Five Teenagers Over Attempted Murder of Iranian Researcher Linked to Opposition
item.description: Five teenage boys, aged between 15 and 17, don start trial for Sweden over the alleged attempted killing of one Iranian researcher, Arvin Khoshnood. Khoshnood na supporter of Reza Pahlavi, the son of ...

item.title: TMC-ECI Meeting Turns Acrimonious: Accusations Fly After Gyanesh Kumar's Remark
item.description: The ongoing tension between the Trinammol Congress (TMC) and the Election Commission of India (ECI) escalated sharply on Wednesday following a particularly heated meeting. A TMC delegation, led by Raj...

item.title: Bulls' missed chances cost them against Glasgow in Champions Cup defeat
item.description: The Bulls suffered a tough 25-21 loss to Glasgow Warriors in the Champions Cup, with coach Johan Ackermann attributing the defeat to missed opportunities and errors under difficult conditions. Ackerma...

item.title: Kurdish Forces Remain Neutral Amid US-Israeli War on Iran Due to Mixed Messages and Iranian Retaliation
item.description: The article discusses how the Kurdish forces, both in Iraq and Iran, were caught in a delicate situation during the US-Israeli war on Iran. Despite initial support from President Trump, the Kurds were...

item.title: Tanzania and Burundi step up voluntary repatriation of refugees under UN supervision
item.description: Tanzania has continued to repatriate Burundian refugees in a process that reflects long-term regional cooperation, changing political conditions in Burundi, and global refugee protection standards. Th...

item.title: Truck traffic at Ambassador Bridge declines as Point Edward gains volume
item.description: Recent traffic data indicates a significant shift in commercial vehicle patterns in Windsor, Ontario. The Ambassador Bridge, long known as Canada’s busiest truck border crossing, has seen a decline in...

item.title: 21-year-old dies in stabbing incident at Primrose Hill, London
item.description: A 21-year-old man, identified as Finbar Sullivan, tragically lost his life following a stabbing at Primrose Hill in north London. The Metropolitan Police reported that Mr Sullivan was discovered near ...

item.title: Challenges Faced by UDF in Retaining Christian Voters in Kerala's 2026 Elections
item.description: With the Kerala Assembly elections fast approaching on April 9, 2026, the UDF (United Democratic Front) faces significant challenges in retaining its traditional Christian voter base, particularly the...

item.title: OPFA to enforce stricter measures on pension funds failing to address member complaints
item.description: Pension funds in South Africa that ignore or delay responses to member complaints may soon face legal and criminal consequences, as the Office of the Pension Funds Adjudicator (OPFA) ramps up enforcem...

item.title: Puducherry assembly elections 2026 to be held tomorrow; two constituencies see major contests
item.description: The Union Territory of Puducherry is all set for its assembly elections tomorrow, 2026. Voters across the region will cast their ballots to elect representatives for the local government. Among the co...

item.title: Coastal Union terminate contract of head coach Mohammed Muya after poor league performance
item.description: Coastal Union football club have officially ended their contract with head coach Mohammed Muya due to disappointing results in the Tanzania Mainland Premier League. The club management and Muya reache...

item.title: Tanzania Plans Regional Training Centre for Railway Personnel to Boost Safety and Skills
item.description: Tanzania, through the Land Transport Regulatory Authority (Latra), is planning to set up a specialised Railway Critical Workers Competency Testing Centre (RCWTC) in Kibaha. The facility, with an estim...

item.title: Cannabis Grow Found in Historic Plas Glynllifon Mansion
item.description: North Wales Police have raided the historic Plas Glynllifon mansion, uncovering a significant cannabis grow operation in the process. The Grade I listed mansion, located near Caernarfon in Gwynedd, ha...

item.title: Senegal and US sign 135‑million‑dollar deal to reform health financing and strengthen universal coverage
item.description: The Senegal government and the United States have signed a five‑year health‑cooperation agreement worth 135 million dollars (2026–2030), under which the US will contribute 63 million dollars and Seneg...

item.title: Philippines plans to boost oil supply during US-Iran ceasefire, says Marcos
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced that the Philippines will take advantage of the recent two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran to secure the country's petroleum suppl...
